title: Financial Accountant
description:
Angel Yeast – Developing Yeast Biotechnology. Innovate for Healthy Life.

Angel Yeast Group Angel Yeast was founded in 1986. It originated from the China National Yeast Research Center and was listed on the Shanghai Stock Exchange in 2000. As one of the leading companies in the worldwide yeast industry, we are committed to the mission of developing yeast biotechnology and innovating for healthy life. Our business activities range from baking to food taste, nutrition & health, and biotechnologies. Our products are available in more than 155 countries and regions. Angel Yeast, Singapore Established in 2021, Angel Yeast Singapore Pte. Ltd. holds the full group business presence in Asia Pacific within regional headquartered functions. We pay close attention to our customers' demands in this region and transform them into company initiatives with better engagement and services. Approach to our customers is one of our core values. We ensure Angel Yeast collaborates with local bakers to create regional trends and features.

Job Summary

This is a key finance position within our Singapore entity, responsible for ensuring accurate financial reporting, maintaining strong internal controls, and supporting the company’s ongoing business growth in the region. You will manage the full spectrum of accounting operations, collaborate closely with cross-functional teams, and serve as a critical link between local activities and the headquarters' financial requirements.

Responsibilities

  • Manage and execute daily accounting operations for the Singapore entity to ensure accurate financial data processing and compliance
  • Support tax compliance activities, warehouse accounting, accounts payable and receivable management, and bank reconciliation to maintain financial accuracy and regulatory adherence
  • Perform month-end closing activities including preparing journal entries, schedules, and conducting financial analysis to deliver timely and accurate financial results
  • Prepare monthly financial statements and management reports to inform leadership decision-making and business strategy
  • Coordinate and complete annual audit and tax filing requirements to meet statutory obligations and maintain transparency
  • Maintain and organize financial records and statutory documents to ensure data integrity and audit readiness
  • Assist in enhancing internal control processes and financial systems to improve operational efficiency and risk management
  • Lead and contribute to ad-hoc finance projects that support business growth and process improvements

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree or higher in Accounting, Finance, or related disciplines
  • Minimum 2 years of relevant financial accounting experience
  • Proficiency in ERP systems and strong computer literacy

Benefits

  • Lunch allowance for working days
  • Career development opportunities
  • Positive and collaborative work environment
